column1: # reference-gates.tsv — L1 cross-version parity audit (epic rust-oracledb-xver-parity-so3w, bead .1)
# Every version/capability gate in reference/python-oracledb thin, mapped to our port.
# Columns 1-3 (reference_file, line, capability_or_constant) are the STABLE gate identity;
# `scripts/extract_reference_gates.sh --check` re-derives them from the live reference and
# fails (exit 3) if a gate appears/vanishes/changes — i.e. after a reference bump, so a NEW
# gate cannot ship unmapped. Columns 4+ are the hand-maintained mapping/coverage.
# The last column (boundary_test) names the OFFLINE, deterministic per-PR unit
# test that flips this gate at its exact boundary (field/protocol version - 1 vs
# version, or capability off vs on) and asserts the conditional bytes are absent
# fails (exit 4) if any non-parity-neutral row lacks a boundary_test that exists
# in the crate sources — so a version gate can never ship without a boundary test,
# even for versions below our live floor (18c, ttc field version 11) that no live
# server can reach. Several gates share one test (it flips >1 field in one build).
#   OK-EXERCISED    mirrored, correct constant+direction, boundary crossed by the live matrix (18c/21c/23ai)
#   OK-UNEXERCISED  mirrored, correct, but the version boundary is BELOW our live floor (18c, ttc field ver 11)
#                   so no live server crosses it — now pinned offline by boundary_test (closes the coverage hole)
#   FIXED           was a missing/wrong gate; fixed this session (semver-additive) + unit regression test
#   PARITY-NEUTRAL  capability for a feature we do not implement (or a client-side/derived flag that is not a
#                   wire-field gate); our conservative path writes NO version-specific bytes, so the gate cannot
#                   corrupt the wire (evidenced non-issue). boundary_test = n/a-parity-neutral, no test required.
# reclassified to PARITY-NEUTRAL in the .1 below-floor sweep (verified non-issues, NOT code bugs):
#   messages/connect.pyx:111 supports_oob in CONNECT — supports_oob is derived from the ACCEPT (capabilities.pyx:121),
#     so it is always false when the CONNECT packet is built; the reference also emits a fixed TNS_GSO_DONT_CARE
#     there. Our CONNECT is byte-identical (proven by the live truth diff). Version-independent; not a gate.
#   protocol.pyx:195,196 supports_bool — a client-side capability flag used to validate/reject PL/SQL BOOLEAN binds,
#     not a conditional wire field. We send the BOOLEAN bind and the server validates it; no field-shift risk.

column9: no (18c floor=11 already >= 11; no sub-11 lane)

column5: skip ub4 sql-type + ub4 server-checksum (return info)

column8: conservative: fixed TNS_GSO_DONT_CARE (supports_oob is unknown pre-ACCEPT, so false at connect)
column9: n/a (byte-identical to reference; see header note)

column5: write al8sqlsig block (5 fields) in EXECUTE
write chunk-ids block (2 fields) in EXECUTE

column8: Large32 for data (Legacy16 for pre-ACCEPT CONNECT); floor 315==MIN_LARGE_SDU

column7: feature not implemented (no DRCP session-state ops)

column8: client-side validation flag, not a wire-field gate (server validates BOOLEAN bind)

column5: (second line of the supports_bool assignment above)
send OOB check probe after connect
send in-band marker when no OOB
